page.content: The table presents data on various sorghum cultivars with specific attributes. In row 1, the cultivar SB10TA from the locality Taarankoukou, harvested in 2010, has a grain color of White, a test weight of 700.05 ± 2.19 g/L, a 100-kernel weight of 3.23 ± 0.17 g, and an endosperm texture comprising 10% corneous, 85% intermediate, and 5% starchy. Row 2 features the cultivar SB10AS, also from 2010 in In Salah, with a White grain color, a test weight of 707.90 ± 3.75 g/L, a 100-kernel weight of 3.25 ± 0.06 g, and an endosperm texture of 80% corneous, 20% intermediate, and 0% starchy. In row 3, the SR10AS cultivar, harvested in 2010 in In Salah, is Red in color, has a test weight of 722.58 ± 3.13 g/L, a 100-kernel weight of 2.77 ± 0.10 g, and an endosperm texture of 25% corneous, 70% intermediate, and 5% starchy. Row 4 showcases SM10AS from 2010 in In Salah, with a grain color of Red and White, a test weight of 718.16 ± 7.16 g/L, a 100-kernel weight of 3.44 ± 0.06 g, and an endosperm texture of 5% corneous, 75% intermediate, and 20% starchy. The cultivar SB11TA, recorded in row 5 for 2011 in Taarankoukou, is White, with a test weight of 708.19 ± 2.62 g/L, a 100-kernel weight of 3.07 ± 0.10 g, and an endosperm texture of 55% corneous, 35% intermediate, and 10% starchy. Row 6 details SB11MA from 2011 in El Malah, also White, with a test weight of 721.34 ± 2.20 g/L, a 100-kernel weight of 2.82 ± 0.09 g, and an endosperm texture of 0% corneous, 80% intermediate, and 20% starchy.

page.content: In row 7, SB12FE from 2012 in Foggarat Ezzoua has a White grain color, a test weight of 733.78 ± 3.01 g/L, a 100-kernel weight of 3.44 ± 0.04 g, and 75% corneous, 25% intermediate, and 0% starchy endosperm texture. Row 8 presents SR12DJ from 2012 in Djafou, Red in color with a test weight of 727.56 ± 8.32 g/L, a 100-kernel weight of 2.87 ± 0.14 g, and an endosperm texture of 0% corneous, 80% intermediate, and 20% starchy. Finally, row 9 includes SR12AS from 2012 in In Salah, which is Red, with a test weight of 727.63 ± 9.61 g/L, a 100-kernel weight of 3.06 ± 0.04 g, and an endosperm texture of 45% corneous, 55% intermediate, and 0% starchy.
